item.title: It's Decided: AA Gaming Is Now Better Than AAA
item.description:
No matter if players are Nintendo, Xbox, or PlayStation fans, they typically look forward to first-party games, which tend to be the tentpoles for any given gaming year. For the most part, big-budget AAA games do tend to rule the year in terms of conversations, but that is not always the case.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 and Hollow Knight: Silksong , for example, were the talk of 2025. Will that hold for Mewgenics and Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection as well? Let's take a look at some AA and indie games from recent memory that often have more juice than AAA games that seemingly have infinite money thrown at their development.

Click or tap on the games that match the category

Category: —
Time: 30s
Combo: 0 • x1.00

Round 1

The category for round 1 is …

5

Results

0
—
More Games

Scott Pilgrim EX

Beat Down In Toronto

Scott Pilgrim EX
8 /10
Released
March 3, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone 10+ / Fantasy Violence, Language, Mild Suggestive Themes, Simulated Gambling, Use of Alcohol
Developer(s)
Tribute Games Inc.
Genre(s)
Beat 'Em Up, Action, Adventure
Powered by

Scott Pilgrim EX is a completely new and original idea in the series, combining characters from across the Scott Pilgrim universe to help save Toronto from invaders. Each character has a unique set of traits. Scott punches and uses his guitar in specials, while Ramona uses a hammer and can summon other objects from her inter-dimensional purse. By defeating enemies, players can use cash in shops to buy items, accessories, and food that will level up stats.

The pixel art is outstanding, along with the music, so even though it’s a simple brawler, Scott Pilgrim EX will easily hold up ten years down the line because of the attention to detail and solid core mechanics.

Pokemon Pokopia

Hanging Out With Nostalgia

Pokemon Pokopia
9 /10
Released
March 5, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone / Users Interact, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Koei Tecmo, Game Freak
Genre(s)
Life Simulation, Simulation
Powered by

Pokemon Pokopia is better than anyone could have predicted for a Pokemon spin-off and a life-sim. As Ditto transforms into a human, players can go around different biomes to set up habitats for other Pokemon to bring them out of hiding. The base-building aspects are simple, and just hanging out with Pokemon is cheerful.

It’s very easy to learn, from punching rocks to gather materials to using strange abilities like squirting water to hydrate plants. Even though it lacks combat, Pokemon Pokopia is one of the best Pokemon games in ages, including the main games.

Mewgenics

Cat Tactics

Mewgenics
Tactical
Turn-Based Strategy
RPG
Systems
Released
February 10, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Edmund McMillen, Tyler Glaiel
Genre(s)
Tactical, Turn-Based Strategy, RPG
Powered by

Mewgenics is pure addition in a tactical package. Players will inevitably want to pull that “one more run” mantra every time they lose. As a cat collector, players find and breed cats to create new hybrids with randomized stats.

On runs, they bring a select team of those cats, fight grid-based battles, and then repeat. Like the best roguelikes, players will find a sense of progression the more they play, which is a lot of work, but it’s satisfying at the same time. Plus, it’s just an absurdly funny game.

Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection

Age Of Dragons

Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection
8 /10
Released
March 13, 2026

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Teen / Fantasy Violence, Mild Suggestive Themes, In-Game Purchases
Developer(s)
Capcom
Genre(s)
RPG, Creature Collector, Exploration, Adventure
Powered by

Like Final Fantasy , new players can jump into Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection without having played any other entry in this spin-off franchise. In a new world, players can fight monsters in turn-based battles alongside allies and monster companions, similar to something like Pokemon, but with human fighters, too.

The colorful world looks like an anime come to life thanks to the cel-shaded visuals, and it can feel like an old-school RPG from the 90s in a good way. Plus, the difficulty is more balanced, and the gameplay is easier to understand than the mainline Monster Hunter games, making Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflect ion a great entry point.

Hollow Knight: Silksong

A Bug’s Life

Hollow Knight: Silksong
Released
September 4, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone 10+ / Fantasy Violence, Mild Blood
Developer(s)
Team Cherry
Genre(s)
Metroidvania, Action, Adventure, Soulslike
Powered by

Hollow Knight: Silksong absolutely rips on so many levels, from the art design to the world-building to the action. Similar to a Soulslike, this 2D Metroidvania will beat down its players on a regular basis. It’s far from an easy experience, but the difficulty spikes are rewarding to overcome.

Eventually, the game will open up more and allow players to visit different areas and upgrade aspects to hopefully get better, then return to older areas later with better abilities. For an indie game, it’s also packed with content, and none of it is filler either. Kudos to Team Cherry for clearly caring about their game and the fans they made it for.

Baby Steps

Left Foot, Right Foot

Baby Steps
Released
September 23, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
Gabe Cuzzillo, Maxi Boch, Bennett Foddy
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Casual, Exploration
Powered by

Baby Steps is not going to be for everyone, and it’s funny to call it the Dark Souls of walking simulators, but it's also an appropriate nickname. Nate is an outcast who gets tossed into a fantasy world where he cannot control his body normally anymore. Players have to walk one foot at a time, and this is not as simple as it sounds.

Imagine a worm trying to drive a tank, and that’s a pretty apt description of the struggles players will have controlling Nate. That said, Nate’s interactions with NPCs are priceless, and the set pieces are incredibly diverse, making Baby Steps a must-play.

Split Fiction

If Only AI Were This Good

Split Fiction
Released
March 6, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
T For Teen // Blood and Gore, Crude Humor, Language, Violence
Developer(s)
Hazelight Studios
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Sci-Fi, Fantasy
Powered by

Split Fiction is just one example of a game from a small studio that has the energy of a company like Activision or Square Enix, but with even more passion. This co-op-only game stars two writers, Mio and Zoe, who get trapped in a simulator tha takes their fantasy and sci-fi ideas and turns them into VR mini-games.

Gameplay settings and mechanics will change on a dime, which helps keep the campaign moving at a brisk pace. From water skiing courses to turning into fantasy creatures and platforming around, Split Fiction is a creative wonder from Hazelight Studios.

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33

The French Connection

Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
Released
April 24, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood and Gore, Strong Language, Suggestive Themes, Violence
Developer(s)
Sandfall Interactive
Genre(s)
Turn-Based RPG, JRPG, Fantasy
Powered by

It’s amazing that Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 was made by a small studio, Sandfall Interactive, and that it was their first game. In many ways, it’s an homage to classics like Super Mario RPG and Final Fantasy, but it feels like a contemporary akin to something like Persona 5 .

The storytelling is deeply dramatic, but has a lot of heart. The active turn-based combat system shows the best of what RPGs can do, and the world design itself is expressive. There’s not a single aspect of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 that feels unfinished, which is why it had so much success during 2025's award season.

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2

A Knight’s Tale

Kingdom Come: Deliverance II
10 /10
Released
February 4, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Use of Alcohol, Blood and Gore, Sexual Content, Strong Language, Intense Violence, Partial Nudity
Developer(s)
Warhorse Studios
Genre(s)
RPG, Action-Adventure, Open-World
Powered by

Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 is an open-world first-person RPG that could easily go head-to-head against classics like The Elder Scrolls 5: Skyrim . Set in Bohemia in the 1400s, this historical RPG lacks magic, but its realistic approach to exploration, combat, and NPC interactions is all generally very well-designed.

Subscribe to the newsletter for smarter AA & indie game picks

Dive deeper by subscribing to the newsletter for curated AA and indie game coverage—thoughtful analysis, developer context, spotlighted hidden gems, and clear takes that help you spot standout non-AAA titles worth playing.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Players will feel every blow they block and every stab they thrust into their opponents. NPCs will realistically react to actions taken, making every choice matter. Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 can feel more like a simulator than an RPG at times, but in a positive way, responding to player actions in a way that feels authentic and memorable.

Dispatch

Better Than Games AND Cartoons?

Dispatch
Released
October 22, 2025

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood, Crude Humor, Intense Violence, Nudity, Sexual Content, Strong Language, Use of Drugs and Alcohol
Developer(s)
AdHoc Studio
Genre(s)
Strategy, Point-and-click, Adventure
Powered by

Players engaging with Dispatch , despite the constant interruptions from dialogue choices, will often forget they are playing a game. They assume the role of a retired superhero, Robert, who is in charge of a low-grade new set of superhero recruits who have to prove themselves worthy of being called heroes.

Robert is wet behind the ears, his clients are disrespectful and unhinged, and balancing when to be kind and when to push back takes a lot of strategy. With excellent storytelling, acting, and animation, Dispatch will feel more like the cousin to animated hits like Invincible than just an ordinary narrative-driven video game.

item.title: 6 Wild Fan Challenges That Make Familiar Games Feel New
item.description:
Fan favorite games like Bloodborne are beloved for a reason. Many games are carefully designed and lovingly balanced to provide a curated player experience. However, once a game is out of the developers' hands, players can find new and innovative ways to manipulate the mechanics, creating a completely different experience. This is especially true of fan challenges that add a new layer of challenge to second or subsequent play-throughs.

Most of these involve completing the full game in an altered or restricted state. But some are all but impossible to complete from a purist perspective. Whether it be a devastating critical path roadblock or a next-to-impossible boss encounter. But that doesn’t stop player after player from attempting these challenges. As a weird completionist who loves pulling apart games to see how they tick, I love a good self-imposed rule, and these are some of the best. Will you take up the Gauntlet?

Bloodborne Sprint Challenge

Ducking and Weaving

The rules:

  • No attacks
  • No kills
  • All running

By the time a player has made it through Bloodborne , they will be incredibly familiar with certain challenging sections. Maps like the Forbidden Woods are notorious for their labyrinthine structure and diverse enemy types. This means that players will tend to die a lot and travel down the same winding paths over and over again. So why not put that otherwise useless geographic knowledge to the test? Basically, the challenge is to run to each shortcut and eventually the boss without killing a single NPC.

As someone who has to get images for Bloodborne and Souls games regularly, I was surprised this was something people were doing… for fun… Unless you are a much better player than me, you may need to do some serious grinding to get past the boss. It is theoretically possible to do a Sprint-only run, only killing bosses if you are skilled enough.

All Ducks Hitman Challenge

Not So Silent Assassin

Hitman World of Assassination
Released
January 20, 2023

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Developer(s)
IO Interactive
Genre(s)
Stealth, Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, PC
OpenCritic Rating
Mighty
Powered by

The rules:

  • Just explosive duck kills
  • No witnesses (including security cameras)
  • No non-target kills

Explosive ducks are a chaotic and iconic staple of Hitman . A favorite of less stealthy hitmen, they come in three main varieties: remote explosive ducks, which detonate via trigger; proximity explosive ducks, which explode when someone nears them; and grenade-style ducks, which detonate after being thrown. They are the best friends of the ‘grim reaper’ or ‘black cat’ hitman.

While they are effective killing tools, they fly in the face of the stealthy ethos of the Silent Assassin. But in this challenge, it is essential to get all the silent assassins with exploding ducks. Subduing with other weapons is allowed, but killing with anything else means starting the level all over again. Without giving too much away, one of the easiest strategies involves triggering a target evacuation.

The Spiffing Brit’s Elder Scrolls Oblivion Dirty Hoe Challenge

Who Can Resist an Insulting Pun?

The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion
Released
March 20, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

ESRB
M for Mature: Blood and Gore, Language, Sexual Themes, Use of Alcohol, Violence
Developer(s)
Bethesda
Genre(s)
RPG
Platform(s)
PC, PS3, Xbox 360
Powered by

The rules:

  • No Magic
  • No advanced weapons
  • Just hoe

This challenge was inspired by a commenter’s sarcastic comment asking if YouTuber The Spiffing Brit could defeat Oblivion with his mom. Thus, the dirty hoe challenge was born. It is an iteration on the classic “player completes X game with only an X” that game breakers like Spiff specialize in. And this time it was the commenter’s mother: a dirty hoe.

Or more accurately, a rusty diseased hoe which inflicts diseases on the target. In true Spiff style, he uses some game-breaking trickery. He starts an orc for melee advantage before getting extremely cheeky with item physics. Spiff is a master at the ridiculous Elder Scrolls challenges, including his most recent ‘Biblically Accurate Skryrim Challenge’.

Nuzlocke Pokémon

Thank You For Your Service, My Friend

The rules:

  • You may only catch the first Pokémon you encounter in an area.
  • All Pokémon must be renamed.
  • Once a Pokémon has fainted, it is considered dead.

The Nuzlocke Pokémon run is not a new fan challenge. It has long been a staple of secondary playthroughs and exists in many variations, each raising the difficulty. At its core, the player may only catch the first Pokémon encountered and must rename it. These rules aim to create an emotional bond, making every catch meaningful because each addition to the team could be lost permanently.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

The player then continues through the game with their Pokémon until it faints. In a Nuzlocke run, this means the Pokémon is now considered dead and can no longer be used in battle. These restrictions are designed not only to make the game more challenging but also to create a stronger emotional connection as the player becomes invested in keeping their Pokémon alive.

World of Warcraft Pacifist Run

Tolstoy Could Never…

World of Warcraft
8 /10
Released
November 23, 2004

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
T for Teen: Blood and Gore, Crude Humor, Mild Language, Suggestive Themes, Use of Alcohol, Violence (online interactions not rated)
Developer(s)
Blizzard
Genre(s)
MMORPG
Platform(s)
PC
Powered by

The rules:

  • See how far you can get in a major quest line without killing ANYTHING.
  • No teaming or grouping with non-pacifist players

To say there is a lot to do in World of Warcraft is an insane understatement. Want to be a sword and shield-wielding hero? You can! Want to be a peaceful fisherman who never touches a quest line? Well, I guess you can do that too. But what if you want to get through a substantial critical questline AND take the pacifist path? That’s when things get trickier.

This challenge essentially involves seeing how far you can get without attacking a single enemy. My introduction to this challenge was through Griffin McElroy’s Peacecraft series about a decade ago, and even then, this is not a new challenge. Like Nuzlocke Pokémon, a Warcraft pacifist run has become a staple of those wanting to mix up a questline or change the way they play on a secondary account.

Anti-capitalist No Merchant Resident Evil Run

What Aren't You Buying, Stranger?

Resident Evil Village
8 /10
Released
May 7, 2021

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Capcom
Genre(s)
Survival Horror, Survival, Horror
Platform(s)
PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, PlayStation VR2, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch 2, Nintendo Switch, PC, iOS
OpenCritic Rating
Mighty
Powered by

The rules:

  • Pick a Resident Evil game with some kind of merchant.
  • Don't buy anything from the merchant or the Duke.
  • Panic!

What’s the quickest way to make a borderline survival horror game more difficult? Why not remove one of the few things that make it more accessible and empowering? One way that Resident Evil 4 and Resident Evil Village stand out from the other entries in the series is the assistance of a vendor to trade goods with. The Duke and the Merchant might not be the most reassuring-looking characters, but they are the closest to friends that Leon and Ethan have on their journeys through hordes of infected villagers.

Resident Evil Village is perhaps the most fun and challenging to try this with, as the Duke has a little more complexity in his wares. And despite his training since Resident Evil 7 , Ethan canonically needs more assistance than Leon to get through the game. But it also means that you have random fish clogging up your inventory.

Fallout 4 Vegan Challenge

Even Mirelurk Meat is Murder

The rules:

  • Don’t eat meat or animal by-products
  • Don’t craft with any meat or animal by-products.
  • Only kill animals in self-defense

With many people revisiting Fallout 4 in the wake of the TV series, it's important to include a little something for those trudging the wastelands once again. Killing animals and crafting items from animals, both benign and aggressive, is a key part of the Fallout experience. But what if the Lone Wanderer was an unfortunate vegan trudging through the radioactive ruins of civilization?

Subscribe to the newsletter for fan-challenge deep dives

Get richer coverage by subscribing to the newsletter: step-by-step strategy breakdowns, challenge setups, and community highlights focused on fan-made game challenges — plus broader gaming coverage for curious players.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

In many vegan challenges, it is possible to complete a game without killing anything, except for the occasional human or intergalactic adversary. But in Fallout 4 , certain compromises need to be made. While killing animals is sometimes unavoidable, the vegan challenge requires the player to only kill in self-defense. This ultimately means that the player must be strong enough to withstand at least one blow from a Deathclaw before they can attack. This is a pretty high bar to clear when you spend most of the game running away from every experience-giving combat situation.

item.title: Pillars of Eternity Gets a Fan-Requested Feature After Years of Waiting
item.description:
It's been more than a decade since Pillars of Eternity originally launched, but the game is finally getting a much-requested turn-based gameplay feature as part of an update, which is available now. The changes give players a reason to dive back into Pillars of Eternity and experience it from a fresh perspective. For anyone who enjoyed Avowed 's world but found the experience lacking, it might be time to give PoE a try.

Originally launching in 2015, Pillars of Eternity kept Obsidian Entertainment afloat following the release of Fallout: New Vegas . The product of a successful Kickstarter campaign, fans played a relatively active role in the game's development, meeting donation milestones that helped it expand into a sprawling RPG. The end product was widely well-received, applauded for its rich narrative and compelling world, helping ignite a sequel, Deadfire, which released in 2018.

Pillars of Eternity Gets Turn-Based Combat

Obsidian Entertainment announced that turn-based combat is now available in Pillars of Eternity , a dramatic change that fundamentally overhauls how players interact with the game. Previously, players could only partake in live combat with a pause mechanic, similar to that of Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic . This change makes the game much more akin to Baldur's Gate, though there are obviously some differences between the two systems, as Pillars of Eternity isn't based on Dungeons and Dragons .

In the new mode, turns are dictated by a character's speed stats, and encounter pacing has been adjusted to be more lethal. Certain actions, like drinking potions and enabling modals, are now classified as free actions with per-turn limits. If all that sounds good, players can either select turn-based mode when starting a new game or toggle it in the options menu, HUD, or with a keybinding. In addition to the new mode, players can now change to the original main menu art by clicking through the base game and DLC icons.

This isn't the first time that the franchise has flirted with turn-based mechanics, as Obsidian added the mode to Pillars of Eternity 2 several years ago. However, Obsidian has tweaked the turn-based mode compared to the sequel's version. Initiative, round length, core stats, and lethality are all different than their Deadfire counterparts. Anyone hoping for a 1:1 recreation may find themselves disappointed.

Find all 10 pairs

Results

0
—
More Games
Find all 10 pairs
Results
0
—
More Games

It's good to see Obsidian get back to its roots with the Pillars of Eternity world. The studio released Avowed early in 2025, but the game received only a lukewarm reception. However, there's plenty to enjoy in the sprawling CRPG for those who appreciate Avowed 's world. With a rich cast of characters and expansive cities to explore, it's a solid next stop for those who enjoyed Baldur's Gate 3 and are looking for something new to chew on.

Hopefully, there's more to come from Obsidian Entertainment in the near future. The studio has been cranking out games at a pretty healthy pace in recent years, with games like Grounded and The Outer Worlds . Fans will have to wait and see what the future holds.

Pillars of Eternity

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 89 /100 Critics Rec: 97%
Released
March 26, 2015
ESRB
M For Mature 17+ due to Blood, Sexual Themes, Strong Language, Violence
Developer(s)
Obsidian Entertainment
Publisher(s)
Paradox Interactive, Versus Evil

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

item.title: Dungeons and Dragons '5.5e' is Official, Sort Of
item.description:
Dungeons and Dragons is officially referring to the 2024 rules refresh of 5th Edition as "5.5e" on D&D Beyond . Many fans already call this new version of Dungeons and Dragons 5.5e, and now, D&D Beyond has followed suit.

In September 2021, Dungeons and Dragons announced a backward-compatible, evergreen evolution of 5th Edition. It originally referred to this project as One D&D , though Dungeons and Dragons eventually retired the name, claiming it did not want the update to feel like a new edition. When it finally launched, the rules refresh was simply referred to as Dungeons and Dragons 5th Edition (2024) . That said, the community swiftly began referring to this version of the rules as "5.5e."

Dungeons and Dragons 2024 Now Called ‘5.5e’ on D&D Beyond

Now, Wizards of the Coast has given up the fight against 5.5e in at least one location. Starting in March 2026, Wizards of the Coast began referring to the 2024 version of 5th Edition as 5.5e on D&D Beyond . While the titles of books have not changed, the rules, options, and materials from these books now carry the 5.5 label in many spots on D&D Beyond , including My Library and the Quickbuilder.

However, according to D&D Beyond ’s FAQ on the matter, this change is not a part of a larger rebrand. Wizards of the Coast is not going to start referring to the 2024 rules as 5.5e outside of D&D Beyond , nor will the titles of the 2024 Player’s Handbook , Monster Manual , Dungeon Master’s Guide , or any new Dungeons and Dragons book releases reflect this categorization change. The 5.5e title is only being used for labeling and filtering on D&D Beyond to make it easier for fans to tell what materials came from which era of 5th Edition.

The 5.5e label itself was derived from 3.5e, a massive update to the 3rd Edition Dungeons and Dragons ruleset released in 2003. Like the 2024 refresh to 5th Edition, this included a new set of core rulebooks, rebalanced player and DM options, and backward compatibility. It could be argued that 5.5e has more drastic changes from 5e than 3.5e did from 3e, so it is no surprise fans jumped on the name. Even so, it took Wizards of the Coast much longer to adopt the 5.5e nomenclature than it did 3.5 – and even then, just on D&D Beyond .

Drag weapons to fill the grid

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.

Time —
Filled 0/36

Results

0
—
More Games
Drag weapons to fill the grid
EasyMediumHard

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.
Time —
Filled 0/36
Results
0
—
More Games

It is important to note that the D&D Beyond FAQ says Wizards of the Coast has no plans to do a larger 5.5e rebrand "for now," implying that it technically could at some point in the future. Indeed, the 2024 rules refresh for 5th Edition was intended to be an evergreen version of D&D, but in another decade or two, Wizards of the Coast could always decide to update the rules again, or possibly even make a new edition of Dungeons and Dragons with even more changes. But for now at least, it seems like Wizards of the Coast has finally given up its crusade against the 5.5e name, even if the company hasn’t universally adopted it.

item.title: Is There Any Real Reason To Keep Your Old PS3 Plugged In?
item.description:
The PS3 launched in 2006, and several models later, it was replaced by the PS4 in 2013. The PS3 had some issues with original models, which led to their failure over time, as if a jet engine were about to take off in one’s living room at a moment’s notice.

That said, the slim model has proven more resilient, and PS3 fans are still using it to play their favorite games that they can’t play legally anywhere else. This includes PS3 games that cannot be streamed on PlayStation Plus. The following games are either widely known masterpieces or hidden gems waiting to get another shot. Either way, they're why fans still own a PS3 and keep it plugged in for occasional use.

GameRant Quiz

—

QUESTION 1/1

Results

0

—

More Games
GameRant Quiz
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)
—
QUESTION 1/1
Results
0
—
More Games

3D Dot Game Heroes

A Voxel To The Past

3D Dot Game Heroes
Action RPG
Adventure
Systems
Released
November 5, 2009

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone 10+ // Mild Cartoon Violence
Developer(s)
Silicon Studio, FromSoftware, Inc.
Genre(s)
Action RPG, Adventure
Powered by

3D Dot Game Heroes is an unsung gem from the PS3 era, having never left the platform in any capacity. It’s an homage to the original Zelda game on the NES, but reimagined as a 3D voxel game. Players can choose a preset avatar, or they can make their own if they are crafty enough to make Cloud from Final Fantasy 7 or, more appropriately, Link from the Zelda series.

Players quest around the overworld, take on tasks, and dive into challenging dungeons filled with monsters. Besides the cool visuals and avatar creation, 3D Dot Game Heroes is known for its absurdly giant swords that can cover entire screens at full health. Part homage, part parody, 3D Dot Game Heroes would get a lot more love if it were ported to any other platform.

Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns Of The Patriots

Nowhere To Hide

Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots
Released
June 12, 2008

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
M For Mature 17+ due to Blood, Crude Humor, Strong Language, Suggestive Themes, Violence
Developer(s)
Kojima Productions
Genre(s)
Stealth
Powered by

Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots was released in 2008, and after almost two decades, it is finally leaving the PS3 in August via Metal Gear Solid: Master Collection Vol. 2 . That said, August is a long way away, and the collection may change some things or even get canceled before launch. That’s why it’s still a good idea to keep MGS4 loaded on the PS3 to see Solid Snake’s final game.

Unlike a lot of Metal Gear games, Metal Gear Solid 4: Guns of the Patriots is more linear than most entries because there are quite a few locations to change up the gameplay. While things are more open in the opening desert town, they become more structured in later areas. The series' iconic blend of action, stealth gameplay, and over-the-top set pieces is not lost, though, as Hideo Kojima gave this one his all as if it were going to be his last.

LittleBigPlanet 2

Stitched Together

LittleBigPlanet 2
8 /10
Released
January 18, 2011

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

ESRB
E For Everyone due to Comic Mischief, Mild Cartoon Violence
Developer(s)
Media Molecule
Genre(s)
Platformer
Powered by

Strangely, no PS3 LittleBigPlanet games exist to stream on PlayStation Plus, although it would admittedly be harder to create levels over shoddy streaming networks, which may be one reason why. Regardless of why, LittleBigPlanet 2 is still a great 2D platformer that players can enjoy alone or with friends in four-player co-op, so long as someone still has a PS3. With tons of costumes and accessories, each player can make their Sackboy their own.

Media Molecule topped itself over the original with more imaginative levels and a greater list of creative tools for players to make nearly any type of game they want, from RPGs to racers. The community, unfortunately, is no longer around, as servers have shut down, but even without the level creator, LittleBigPlanet 2 is fun as a strict 2D platformer. Even though it isn’t as widely known as a Mario game, LittleBigPlanet 2 can still offer a great challenge for a party setting.

Killzone 2

Not Quite A Halo Killer

Killzone 2
FPS
Systems
Released
February 26, 2009
ESRB
m
Developer(s)
Guerrilla Games

Genre(s)
FPS
Powered by

Killzone 2 was promoted as a " Halo Killer" back in the day, and while it did not usurp the Halo games on the Xbox 360, or even look as good as the initial teaser trailer, it still turned out to be a cool shooter. The iconic headgear of the Helghast soldiers still haunts fans to this day, and unlike many first-person shooters of this era, the gameplay was very weighty.

The hero moved like a tank, but in turn, they also had steady aim like a well-oiled machine. The color palette is mixed with a lot of blacks and browns, but Killzone 2 makes up for the drab color scheme with an exciting campaign full of bombastic moments. As a first-party series, it’s also strange that no Killzone game from the PS3 era is available to stream on PlayStation Plus.

Twisted Metal (2012)

One Last Hurrah

Twisted Metal (2012)
7 /10
Released
February 12, 2012

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
M // Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Strong Language
Publisher(s)
Sony Interactive Entertainment
Genre(s)
Vehicular Combat
Powered by

Twisted Metal was a reboot released in 2012 that gave players more freedom than past entries. The core gameplay remained the same: players could select characters to go out and fight in car combat arenas. The big difference this time was that there was also a lot more on-the-ground combat with human characters, too.

While not praised as the best Twisted Metal game in the series, it was a nice return after some years of absence, which unfortunately did not earn any more sequels. The only thing close now is the TV show on Peacock, which is honestly a good video game adaptation. The online servers are shut down, but Twisted Metal is still fun alone.

Folklore

An Irish Action RPG

Folklore
Action RPG
Systems
Released
June 21, 2007

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
T for Teen
Developer(s)
game republic
Genre(s)
Action RPG
Powered by

Folklore is a completely forgotten action RPG that never received a port or sequel on the PS3. Players control two different characters, each with the power to capture and summon creatures. They could assign these creatures to the face buttons to attack enemies in real-time combat, almost like a cross between Pokemon and a darker, fairy tale-infused game like The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt .

The Celtic lore inspirations for the monster designs and world helped Folklore stand out, and the combat, while not extraordinary, was satisfying to control. It’s one of the more obscure action RPGs on the PS3, and if given the chance, picking this one up in the wild is recommended. The developer, Game Republic, was not appreciated enough in their brief window of making games, with Folklore being one of their best.

Katamari Forever

Keep Rollin', Rollin', Rollin'

Katamari Forever
Puzzle
Action
Systems
Released
April 30, 2009

WHERE TO PLAY

PHYSICAL

ESRB
Everyone // Alcohol and Tobacco Reference, Comic Mischief, Mild Fantasy Violence, Mild Language
Developer(s)
Namco Bandai
Genre(s)
Puzzle, Action
Powered by

Katamari Forever is one of the coolest entries in the series, and it’s unbelievable that it is trapped on the PS3. Like all Katamari games, players will start as the Prince of the Cosmos, or one of the many cousins, and will go around to different landscapes and roll up objects. Instead of creating stars as the sole focus, this time, players will have to roll up objects to bring color back to the environments.

Subscribe to the newsletter for PS3-only game discoveries

Subscribing to the newsletter delivers curated coverage of PS3-only classics and overlooked gems, with preservation context and must-play recommendations so you can keep discovering why fans still treasure these consoles.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

The color palette is a bit more faded, and the cel-shaded art style is lighter by design, making Katamari Forever stand out even more. The art style is distinct, the music is great, and the gameplay, while challenging to control at first, is easy to get used to. Hopefully, this oddity eventually gets the remaster treatment someday, as other Katamari games have.
